item.title: Bacchus Dionysus
item.description:
god of wine twice born Bacchus the son of Jupiter and Semele represents not only the intoxicating power of wine，but its social and beneficent influences is viewed as the promoter of civilization，and a lawgiver(立法者) and lover of peace
his wife Ariadne
典故 bacchanal 酒鬼 bacchic 烂醉如泥 bacchant n. 酒神的祭司；酒神追随者；狂饮作乐者adj. 崇拜酒神的；狂饮作乐的；嗜酒的 bacchanalian 酒神节的 son of bacchus 男酒鬼 daughter of bacchus 女酒鬼

item.title: Elysian Plain
item.description: 极乐净土 On the western margin of the earth，by the stream of Ocean，lay a happy place named the Elysian Plain，whither mortals favored by the gods were transported without tasting of death，to enjoy an immortality of bliss．This happy region was also called the“Fortunate Fields ，”and the“Isles(岛) of the Blessed．

item.title: Golden Silver Brazen Iron Ages and the Great Flood
item.description:
典故 “The first age was an age of innocence and happiness, called theGolden Age. Truth and right prevailed, though not enforced by law... The earth brought forth all things necessary for man, without his labor.” 和平富足：无需劳作，大地自动产出食物，四季如春，河流流奶与酒，橡树滴蜂蜜。 道德完美：无法律约束，无战争、疾病或衰老，人与神和谐共处。 统治神：克洛诺斯（Saturn）掌权，故又称 “萨图恩时代”。 "Jupiter shortened the spring, and divided the year into seasons... The farmer was obliged to sow the seed and the toiling ox to draw the plough.” 四季初现：宙斯缩短春天，人类首次经历寒暑，需建屋御寒。 劳作开始：不再依赖自然馈赠，首次耕地播种，牛拉犁成为必要。 道德衰退：仍存善念，但已不如黄金时代纯粹。 “Next came the Brazen Age, more savage of temper, and readier to the strife of arms, yet not altogether wicked.” 好战本性：人类变得勇猛好斗，热衷兵器，但尚未完全邪恶。 金属使用：以青铜制造武器，象征力量与暴力的萌芽。 “Crime burst in like a flood; modesty, truth, and honor fled... Men were not satisfied with what the surface produced, but must dig into its bowels and draw forth the ores of metals.” 罪恶泛滥：欺诈、贪婪、暴力横行，甚至亲子相残，道德彻底崩塌。 自然反噬：人类挖掘矿产（铁与金），引发战争，大地荒芜，神祇弃世。 终结事件：宙斯因人类堕落降大洪水，仅丢卡利翁与皮拉幸存。
Zeus, angered by humanity’s depravity, sent a flood. Only Deucalion (Prometheus’ descendant) and his wife Pyrrha survived on Mount Parnassus. An oracle commanded them to cast "their mother’s bones"—interpreted as stones—behind them. These stones transformed into humans: those thrown by Deucalion became men, and those by Pyrrha became women, founding a new, hardier race. 黑铁时代末期，宙斯以洪水灭世，仅丢卡利翁（普罗米修斯之子）与妻子皮拉幸存。 二人遵神谕掷石造人：丢卡利翁的石头变男人，皮拉的石头变女人，开启新人类时代。
item.guid: My notes/English/希腊罗马神话/subnotes/Golden Silver Brazen Iron Ages and the Great Flood.md

item.title: How ancient Greeks see the world 古希腊人的世界观
item.description:
The ancient Greek believe that The earth to be flat and circular their own country occupying the middle of it; the central point being either Mount Olympus the earth was crossed from west to east and divided into two equal parts by the Sea the Mediterranean(地中海) and its continuation the Euxine（黑海）
Around the earth flowed the River Ocean
in the north Hyperboreans 北国人
in the south Æthiopians(埃塞俄比亚人)
in the west Elysian Plain
how the Sun Moon moves(日月星辰的运转)
item.guid: My notes/English/希腊罗马神话/subnotes/How ancient Greeks see the world 古希腊人的世界观.md

item.title: Jupiter

the King and father of all gods and men the ruler of the sky(heaven) and thunder his weapon is thunderbolt his shield called Egis (Vulcan made it) Eagle is his favorite bird
his messenger is Mercury the most powerful god
the husband of Hera Juno
the one who overthrew his father (Saturn Cronos)

item.title: Mercury
item.description:
the son of Jupiter and Maia represents commerce，wrestling，and other gymnastic exercises，even over thieving, in fact, everything which required skill and dexterity(熟练，机敏) wore a winged cap and winged shoes had a rod entwined with two serpents,called the caduceus(双蛇杖)
invented the lyre which made from a tortoise and gave it to Apollo who gave him caduceus(双蛇杖) in exchange

item.title: Mount Olympus
item.description: the abode(住所) of the gods The abode of the gods was on the summit of Mount Olympus，in Thessaly(色萨利) ．A gate of clouds，kept by the goddesses named the Seasons， opened to permit the passage of the Celestials（神，天的，天空的） to earth，and to receive them on their return．The gods had their separate dwellings；but all， when summoned（召集），repaired to the palace of Jupiter，as did also those deities（神） whose usual abode was the earth ， the waters ， or the underworld king ．It was also in the great hall of the palace of the Olympian that the gods feasted each day onambrosia and nectar，their food and drink，the latter being handed round by the lovely goddess Hebe．
item.guid: My notes/English/希腊罗马神话/subnotes/Mount Olympus.md

item.title: Muses 缪斯女神
item.description: They presided over（掌管） song and prompted the memory nine in total  to each of whom was assigned the presidence over some particular department of literature , art , or science Calliope was the muse of epic poetry， Clio of history Euterpe of lyric poetry Melpomene of tragedy Terpsichore of choral dance and song  Erato of love poetry  Polyhymnia of sacred poetry  Urania of astronomy Thalia of comedy．
item.guid: My notes/English/希腊罗马神话/subnotes/Muses 缪斯女神.md

item.title: Venus Aphrodite
item.description:
the goddess of love(偏向性爱) and beauty venereal adj. 性交的；性病的；由性交传染的 daughter of Jupiter and Dione or come from Saturn's dick in the foam(泡沫) of sea Others say that Venus sprang from the foam of the sea．The Zephyr wafted her along the waves to the Isle of Cyprus ,where she was received and attired by the Seasons，and then led to the assembly of the gods．
her husband is Vulcan but she always has relation with others (Jupiter gave her to Vulcan, to thank Vulcan for making thunderbolts for him) her favoirte birds are dove and swan the plants sacred to her is rose and myrtle(桃金娘)

item.title: Vulcan
item.description:
the celestials artist(工匠之神) son of Jupiter and Hera Juno born lame
Juno hates him He was born lame，and his mother(Juno) was so displeased at the sight of him that she flung him out of heaven ． Other accounts say that Jupiter kicked him out for taking part with his mother in a quarrel which occurred between them．Vulcan’s lameness，according to this account, was the consequence of his fall．[102] He was a whole day falling，and at last alighted in the island of Lemnos ，which was thenceforth sacred to him．
his wife is Venus Hephaestus in greek

item.title: 故事的整合,概括
item.description:
Before creation, all existed as Chaos—a formless, weighty mass where the seeds of all things slumbered. At the first, everthing were mixed, then sky first formed and the air next, earth sank below, water took the lowest place. Then some unkonwn gods created everything rivers, mountains, valleys, animals, plants.
Prometheus is one of the titans, after his brother Epimetheus creates many animals, he creates human beings by water and earth in god's image and granting them an upright stance to gaze at the stars. Prometheus, aided by Minerva, stole fire from the sun’s chariot, enabling humans to craft tools, cultivate land, and develop civilization.
To punish Prometheus for stealing fire from heaven and giving it to mankind, Jupiter created the first woman,Pandora, and sent her to earth as a trap. Each god contributed to her creation—Venus gave her beauty, Mercury gave her persuasion, and Apollo gave her music—making her irresistibly charming. Though warned by his brother Prometheus to be wary of Jupiter’s gifts, Epimetheus accepted Pandora as his wife. In his house stood a jar containing harmful things that had not been needed when man was first created. Overcome by curiosity, Pandora opened the jar, releasing lots of miseries into the world—diseases like gout and colic, and emotional afflictions such as envy and revenge. She hastily tried to close it, but only one thing remained inside: hope. Golden Silver Brazen Iron Ages and the Great Flood
典故 “The first age was an age of innocence and happiness, called theGolden Age. Truth and right prevailed, though not enforced by law... The earth brought forth all things necessary for man, without his labor.” 和平富足：无需劳作，大地自动产出食物，四季如春，河流流奶与酒，橡树滴蜂蜜。 道德完美：无法律约束，无战争、疾病或衰老，人与神和谐共处。 统治神：克洛诺斯（Saturn）掌权，故又称 “萨图恩时代”。 "Jupiter shortened the spring, and divided the year into seasons... The farmer was obliged to sow the seed and the toiling ox to draw the plough.” 四季初现：宙斯缩短春天，人类首次经历寒暑，需建屋御寒。 劳作开始：不再依赖自然馈赠，首次耕地播种，牛拉犁成为必要。 道德衰退：仍存善念，但已不如黄金时代纯粹。 “Next came the Brazen Age, more savage of temper, and readier to the strife of arms, yet not altogether wicked.” 好战本性：人类变得勇猛好斗，热衷兵器，但尚未完全邪恶。 金属使用：以青铜制造武器，象征力量与暴力的萌芽。 “Crime burst in like a flood; modesty, truth, and honor fled... Men were not satisfied with what the surface produced, but must dig into its bowels and draw forth the ores of metals.” 罪恶泛滥：欺诈、贪婪、暴力横行，甚至亲子相残，道德彻底崩塌。 自然反噬：人类挖掘矿产（铁与金），引发战争，大地荒芜，神祇弃世。 终结事件：宙斯因人类堕落降大洪水，仅丢卡利翁与皮拉幸存。
Zeus, angered by humanity’s depravity, sent a flood. Only Deucalion (Prometheus’ descendant) and his wife Pyrrha survived on Mount Parnassus. An oracle commanded them to cast "their mother’s bones"—interpreted as stones—behind them. These stones transformed into humans: those thrown by Deucalion became men, and those by Pyrrha became women, founding a new, hardier race. 黑铁时代末期，宙斯以洪水灭世，仅丢卡利翁（普罗米修斯之子）与妻子皮拉幸存。 二人遵神谕掷石造人：丢卡利翁的石头变男人，皮拉的石头变女人，开启新人类时代。 Prometheus’ Eternal Torment
Prometheus stole fire and taught human lots of things which annoying Zeus, as a punishment, Prometheus was chained to a rock, where an eagle daily devoured his liver which will regenerate soon after being eaten. He refused to reveal a secret that could secure Zeus’ throne, which might make Zeus release him from the punishment. 普罗米修斯用泥土造人，赋予其直立行走的能力，并从太阳神处盗火赠予人类。宙斯为惩罚人类，创造潘多拉（Pandora），她打开魔盒释放疾病、嫉妒等灾祸，仅留下 “希望” 在盒底。人类经历黄金、白银、青铜、铁器时代，铁器时代罪恶横行，宙斯遂降洪水灭世，仅丢卡利翁（Deucalion）和皮拉（Pyrrha）因虔诚幸存，按神谕掷石造人，成为新人类的祖先。普罗米修斯因盗火被宙斯绑在高加索山，每日被鹰啄食肝脏，后被赫拉克勒斯解救。
After the Great Flood, the fertile earth spawned(滋养出) Python, a monstrous serpent lurking in Mount Parnassus. Apollo slew Python with his arrows, marking his first major conquest (previously used only for small game). To commemorate this victory, he founded the Pythian Games, where winners were crowned with beech（山毛榉） wreaths (laurel was not yet his symbol).
Cupid, offended by Apollo’s mockery(嘲笑), shot him with a golden love arrow and Daphne, a nymph, with a leaden arrow which repel love from her. Apollo fell desperately in love with Daphne, who fled from him. As he pursued her, Daphne begged her father, the river god Peneus, to change her form. She transformed into a laurel tree. Apollo declared the laurel his sacred tree, vowing to wear its leaves as a crown, decorate his lyre and quiver with it, and use it in triumphal wreaths for Roman conquerors. The laurel bowed in gratitude.In Babylonia(古巴比伦), Pyramus and Thisbe, forbidden to marry by their parents. They communicated through a crack in the wall between their houses. They planned to meet at the Tomb of Ninus under a white mulberry tree. Thisbe arrived first but fled from a bloodied lioness, dropping her veil. Pyramus found the veil torn and assumed Thisbe was dead. Grief-stricken, he killed himself, his blood staining the mulberries red. Thisbe, discovering his body, stabbed herself with his sword. Their parents buried them together, and the mulberry tree forever bore purple berries.
Cephalus a hunter. One day, the dawn goddess Aurora saw him and fell in love. She took him away, but Cephalus, deeply in love with his wife Procris, and refused her. Procris, a favorite of the goddess Diana, had given Cephalus a magical dog that could outrun anything and a javelin(标枪) that never missed. Aurora, upset by his rejection, warned him he would regret his devotion to Procris. Later, a fierce fox terrorized the land, and Cephalus’s dog was sent to catch it. The chase was so intense that the gods turned both the dog and the fox into stone to prevent either from winning. Cephalus continued hunting alone, and during his rest, he would call out to the breeze to cool him. Someone overheard him and wrongly assumed he was speaking to a lover, then told Procris. Jealous and heartbroken, she followed him one day and hid in the bushes. When Cephalus heard a noise, he thought it was an animal and threw his javelin—accidentally striking Procris. As she lay dying, she begged him not to marry the “odious Breeze,” revealing the tragic misunderstanding. Though Cephalus tried to explain, it was too late—Procris died, forgiving him with her final breath. Apollo and Daphne：阿波罗因嘲笑丘比特，被丘比特用金箭射中爱上达芙妮（Daphne），而达芙妮被铅箭射中厌恶爱情。达芙妮为逃避阿波罗，向父亲河神求助，被变为月桂树，阿波罗遂以月桂为冠。 Pyramus and Thisbe：巴比伦青年皮拉摩斯与提斯柏相爱，因父母反对私会，提斯柏被狮子惊吓遗落面纱，皮拉摩斯误以为她已死，自刎而亡，提斯柏见其尸亦自杀，两人化为桑葚树。 Cephalus and Procris：刻法罗斯（Cephalus）被曙光女神爱慕，却忠于妻子普罗克里斯（Procris）。因误会，刻法罗斯误将普罗克里斯当作野兽射死，追悔莫及。
When Latona who fleeing Juno’s persecution and jealousy, sought water in a Lycian pond where local rustics rudely denied her and defiled(弄脏) the water. Enraged, she cursed them to live in the pond forever as frogs. The curse transformed them: their bodies became bloated, voices croaky, and they were condemned to croak in the slime, a punishment for their cruelty and disrespect toward the goddess. 拉托娜（Latona）是阿波罗与阿尔忒弥斯的母亲，因口渴向吕西亚农民求水，遭拒绝和侮辱。她诅咒农民变为青蛙，永远生活在池塘中，只能发出嘶哑的叫声。
Phaëton, the son ofApollo and nymph Clymene, is mocked by a schoolfellow for claiming divine parentage. he travels to Apollo's palace to seek confirmation. Apollo confirms Phaëton is his son and offers any gift, swearing by a sacred lake. Phaëton insists on driving the Sun's chariot for a day, despite Apollo's fervent warnings about its danger. Reluctantly, Apollo agrees, advising Phaëton to hold the reins tight and stay in the middle course.
When Phaëton takes the chariot, the horses, sensing a lighter load, bolt from the path. Unable to control them, Phaëton causes chaos: the earth burns, mountains ignite, rivers dry up, deserts form (like Libya), Ethiopia turns black, and even the underworld is threatened. Earth pleads with Zeus, who hurls a thunderbolt, striking Phaëton to dead; he falls into the Eridanus River. Phaëton's sisters, the Heliades, mourn him endlessly and transformed into poplar trees(白杨树), their tears becoming amber as tear太阳神阿波罗之子法厄同（Phaëton）为证明身份，恳求驾驶太阳战车，阿波罗劝阻无效。法厄同因失控导致大地燃烧，宙斯用雷霆将其击落，法厄同坠入厄里达诺斯河。其姐妹化为白杨树，眼泪成琥珀。
Midas, the king of Phrygia, once sheltered Silenus, the old tutor and foster-father of Bacchus, for ten days and nights with great hospitality. As a reward, Bacchus granted Midas a wish, and Midas asked that everything he touched turn to gold. Initially delighted, he soon fell into despair: bread, wine, and even water turned to gold at his touch, threatening him with starvation. He begged Bacchus to take back the gift, and the god instructed him to wash in the River Pactolus. Midas did so, and the golden touch transferred to the river’s sands, which remain golden to this day.
Later, Midas judged a music contest between Pan and Apollo. Pan played rustic melodies on his pipes, while Apollo performed on his lyre. Midas unfairly favored Pan, angering Apollo, who cursed him with donkey ears as punishment. Ashamed, Midas hid his ears with a turban, but his barber discovered the secret. Unable to keep it, the barber dug a hole in a meadow, whispered the truth into it, and covered it. Reeds grew from the spot, and whenever the wind blows, they whisper the secret of Midas’ ears.
Jupiter and Mercury, disguised as mortal travelers, wandered Phrygia seeking shelter but were rejected by all except Baucis and Philemon, an elderly couple who lived in a humble cottage. The couple treated the guests with great kindness: they lit a fire, prepared a simple meal of herbs, bacon, and wine, and shared their meager provisions. During the meal, the wine in the pitcher miraculously refilled itself, revealing the guests’ divinity. The gods revealed themselves, announcing that the inhospitable village would be punished, but Baucis and Philemon would be spared. They led the couple to a hill, where the village was flooded, leaving only the couple’s cottage, which transformed into a magnificent temple. Jupiter offered them any wish, and they asked to be priests of the temple and to die at the same time. Their wish was granted. Years later, as they stood before the temple, Baucis and Philemon were transformed into a linden tree and an oak tree, growing side by side, their branches intertwined—a lasting symbol of their love and piety. Midas：迈达斯（Midas）因救酒神巴克斯的老师，获 “点金术”，却因食物、饮水皆成黄金而痛苦，最终在帕科托罗斯河洗净魔力，后因评判潘神与阿波罗的音乐比赛不公，被变成驴耳，秘密被理发师埋入地下，长出的芦苇会低语此事。 Baucis and Philemon：老夫妇博西斯（Baucis）和菲利蒙（Philemon）热情款待伪装成凡人的宙斯与赫尔墨斯，众神为回报其善，将他们的茅屋变为神庙，二人愿同生共死，最终化为并排的椴树与橡树。
Proserpine, daughter of Ceres (goddess of grain), was playing in a flower-filled valley when Pluto, ruler of the underworld, fell in love with her (after Cupid shot him with a love arrow) and carried her away.
Ceres searched everywhere for her daughter, so heartbroken that she made the earth barren—crops failed, land withered. When she learned Proserpine was in the underworld, she begged Jupiter to help. Jupiter agreed to return Proserpine only if she’d eaten no food there. But Proserpine had eaten a few pomegranate(石榴) seeds from Pluto, so a deal have to be compromised: she’d spend half the year with Ceres (bringing spring/summer, when the earth flourishes) and half with Pluto (bringing autumn/winter, when Ceres grieves and the earth rests). 冥王哈迪斯爱上谷物女神刻瑞斯之女珀耳塞福涅（Proserpine），将其掳至冥界。刻瑞斯悲痛欲绝，致使大地荒芜。宙斯裁决珀耳塞福涅半年在人间，半年在冥界，由此形成四季轮回。
Pygmalion, a sculptor, grew to abhor women due to their flaws and resolved to live unmarried. He crafted a stunning ivory statue of a maiden, so lifelike that he fell deeply in love with his creation. He adorned it with gifts, dressed it in fine robes, and treated it as his wife. During the festival of Venus in Cyprus, he prayed at her altar for a wife "like his ivory virgin." Venus, moved by his devotion, granted his wish: the statue came to life. Pygmalion named her Galatea, and they married. Their daughter, Paphos, gave her name to the Cypriot city sacred to Venus. 塞浦路斯国王皮格马利翁（Pygmalion）雕刻象牙少女像，因过于迷恋，祈求维纳斯赋予其生命。维纳斯应允，雕像变为伽拉忒亚（Galatea），二人成婚，女儿帕福斯以其名命名城市，塞浦路斯信仰Venus。
Cadmus, son of Agenor, was tasked with finding his sister Europa, who had been abducted by Jupiter in the form of a bull. Unable to locate her, he consulted Apollo’s oracle, which instructed him to follow a cow and build a city where it rested. The cow led him to Boeotia, where he founded Thebes. To sacrifice to Jupiter, he sent servants to fetch water from a sacred grove, but they were killed by a giant serpent (sacred to Mars). Cadmus slew the serpent and, on a divine command, sowed its teeth, which sprouted into armed warriors. These warriors fought each other until only five remained, who helped build Thebes.
Cadmus married Harmonia (daughter of Venus), but a curse from killing Mars’ serpent plagued his family. After many tragedies, he and Harmonia fled to the Enchelians, where they were made rulers. Grief-stricken by their children’s misfortunes, Cadmus wished to become a serpent, and both he and Harmonia were transformed into serpents—harmless and mindful of their origin. Cadmus is also said to have introduced the Phoenician alphabet to Greece.
The Myrmidons, elite soldiers of Achilles in the Trojan War, traced their origin to a plague in Ægina which ruled by Æacus. A pestilence(瘟疫)) sent by Juno devastated the island, killing nearly all inhabitants. Æacus prayed to Jupiter, who, in response, transformed a colony of ants (myrmex in Greek) into humans. These "Myrmidons" were hardworking and loyal, becoming Achilles’ fearsome warriors. Cadmus：卡德摩斯（Cadmus）为寻被宙斯掳走的妹妹欧罗巴，按神谕随母牛建底比斯城。他杀死战神的毒蛇，将蛇牙播种后长出武装战士，互相残杀后仅五人存活，助其建城。后因杀Mars的蛇获诅咒，与妻子哈耳摩尼亚化为蛇。 The Myrmidons：埃阿科斯（Æacus）的王国遭瘟疫，他祈求宙斯，宙斯将蚂蚁变为密耳弥冬人（Myrmidons），成为阿喀琉斯的精锐部队。
Nisus, king of Megara, had a purple lock of hair that ensured the city’s safety. His daughter Scylla fell in love with Minos, king of Crete, who was besieging Megara. Hoping to win Minos’ favor, Scylla cut off her father’s purple lock while he slept. With the lock gone, Megara fell to the Cretans. Minos, disgusted by her treachery(不忠), rejected Scylla. Heartbroken, she clung to his ship as it sailed away. Nisus, transformed into a sea eagle by the gods, attacked her; in pity, the gods turned Scylla into a bird. To this day, the sea eagle pursues Scylla’s bird form, a permanent reminder of her betrayal. 尼索斯（Nisus）的紫色头发是迈加拉城不败的象征，其女斯库拉（Scylla）爱上克里特国王米诺斯，割下父亲头发，致使城市陷落。米诺斯鄙视其背叛，斯库拉被父亲化为海鸟，尼索斯化为海鹰，永世追逐。
Minerva, goddess of wisdom, contested with Arachne, a mortal weaver who too proud for her skill. Arachne boasted she could beat the goddess in weave and chose to depict(描述) the gods’ misdeeds(罪行) (e.g., Jupiter’s infidelities(不忠)) in her tapestry. Minerva, angered by the insult, tore Arachne’s work and transformed her into a spider, condemning her to weave forever.
Niobe, queen of Thebes and wife of Amphion, arrogantly boasted that her seven sons and seven daughters made her superior to Latona (mother of Apollo and Diana). She mocked Latona’s worship, urging the Thebans to abandon it. In wrath, Latona sent Apollo and Diana to punish her: Apollo kill all seven sons, and Diana killed all seven daughters. Niobe, devastated, turned to stone from grief, but her tears continued to flow. The stone was carried to Mount Sipylus, where it weeps to this day, its tears forming a stream. Minerva：雅典娜（Minerva）与阿拉克涅（Arachne）比赛纺织，阿拉克涅织出神祇丑闻，雅典娜将其变为蜘蛛。 Niobe：底比斯王后尼俄柏（Niobe）因炫耀七子七女，遭勒托之子阿波罗与阿尔忒弥斯射杀全部子女，尼俄柏化为流泪的石像。
Perseus and Medusa: Perseus, the son of Jupiter and Danaë, was sent to defeat Medusa, a monster with snakes for hair whose gaze could turn anyone into stone. With the help of Minerva and Mercury, who lent him her shield and his winged shoes respectively, Perseus approached Medusa while she slept. He avoided looking directly at her, using the reflection in the shield to guide him, and cut off her head. After the victory, he used Medusa's head to turn his enemies into stone. Later, he saved Andromeda, who was chained to a rock as a sacrifice to a sea - monster. Perseus defeated the monster and married Andromeda.
Atlas: After killing Medusa, Perseus flew to the realm of King Atlas. Atlas, who was rich in flocks and herds and had a garden with golden apples, remembered a prophecy that a son of Jove would rob him of his apples. So he tried to drive Perseus away. Perseus, in response, showed him Medusa's head, and Atlas was turned into a mountain that holds up the heavens. His beard and hair became forests, his arms and shoulders cliffs, his head a summit, and his bones rocks. Perseus and Medusa：珀尔修斯（Perseus）在雅典娜与赫尔墨斯帮助下,通过盾牌的反光防止被石化，成功斩杀美杜莎（Medusa），之后用其头颅石化敌人，后解救被海怪威胁的安德洛墨达（Andromeda），二人成婚。 Atlas：珀尔修斯用美杜莎头颅将阿特拉斯（Atlas）化为支撑天空的山脉。
The Sphinx was a monster with the body of a lion and the upper part of a woman. It haunted in the highroad near Thebes and posed a riddle to all passers - by: "What animal is that which in the morning goes on four feet, at noon on two, and in the evening upon three?" Those who couldn't solve it were killed. Many had failed until Œdipus came. He answered correctly, saying "Man", as a baby crawls on all fours, an adult walks on two feet, and an old person uses a staff. The Sphinx, mortified at the solving of her riddle, cast herself down from the rock and perished. Œdipus became the king of Thebes as a result. However, he later unknowingly killed his father and married his mother. When the truth was revealed, he was filled with horror. He tore out his eyes and wandered away from Thebes, a broken man. 忒拜城斯芬克斯（Sphinx）以谜语 “早晨四足、中午两足、晚上三足的动物” 问倒众人，俄狄浦斯（Œdipus）答 “人”，斯芬克斯羞愧自尽，俄狄浦斯成为忒拜王，却不知自己杀父娶母，最终自毁双目流浪。
Jason, the son of Æson, was asked by his uncle Pelias to retrieve the Golden Fleece, which was in the kingdom of Colchis. Jason assembled a band of heroes, the Argonauts, and they set sail on the Argo. When they reached Colchis, the king, Æetes, agreed to give up the fleece if Jason could yoke two fire - breathing bulls, sow the teeth of a dragon (from which armed men would spring up), and overcome them. Medea, the daughter of Æetes and a sorceress, fell in love with Jason. She helped him to complete the tasks. Jason successfully obtained the Golden Fleece and returned to Thessaly with Medea. But later, Jason betrayed Medea and intended to marry Creusa. Enraged, Medea took revenge by sending a poisoned robe to Creusa, killing their children, and setting fire to the palace. She then fled in a serpent - drawn chariot. 伊阿宋（Jason）为夺回金羊毛，率阿尔戈英雄远航，美狄亚（Medea）爱上伊阿宋，助其通过毒牛、龙牙战士等考验，取得金羊毛。后伊阿宋背叛美狄亚，美狄亚杀子复仇，乘蛇车离去。
Meleager: A wild boar, sent by Diana as punishment for Œneus' neglect in offering sacrifices, laid waste to the fields of Calydon. Meleager called on heroes from Greece to hunt the boar. Among them was Atalanta, the daughter of Iasius. Meleager saw and loved her. During the hunt, Atalanta was the first to wound the boar, and Meleager eventually killed it. He gave the head and hide of the boar to Atalanta as a trophy. This angered Meleager's uncles, Plexippus and Toxeus, who tried to take the trophy from her. In a fit of rage, Meleager killed his uncles. His mother, Althea, in grief and seeking revenge, burned a brand that was linked to Meleager's life. Meleager died, and Althea, overcome with sorrow, killed herself.
Atalanta: Atalanta, fearing marriage as predicted by an oracle, imposed a condition on her suitors: she would marry the one who could defeat her in a race, but those who lost would be put to death. Hippomenes, inspired by Venus, asked for her help. Venus gave him three golden apples. During the race, Hippomenes dropped the apples one by one, distracting Atalanta and enabling him to win. However, the couple forgot to pay due honor to Venus, so the goddess punished them by turning them into lions. Meleager：卡吕冬野猪肆虐，墨勒阿革洛斯（Meleager）邀众英雄狩猎，杀死野猪后将皮赠予阿塔兰塔（Atalanta），因舅舅反对，墨勒阿革洛斯杀舅舅，母亲阿尔泰亚为复仇焚烧象征其生命的木柴，墨勒阿革洛斯死亡，母亲自尽。 Atalanta：阿塔兰塔根据神谕以赛跑选夫，输者死，希波墨涅斯（Hippomenes）用维纳斯的金苹果引其分心，获胜后因不敬神被变为狮子。
Hercules: Hercules, the son of Jupiter and Alcmena, was constantly opposed by Juno. He was made to perform twelve difficult labors by Eurystheus. These included killing the Nemean lion, slaying the Hydra, cleaning the Augean stable, and obtaining the girdle of the Amazon queen. After completing these labors, he accidentally killed his friend Iphitus and was condemned to be the slave of Queen Omphale for three years. Later, his wife Dejanira, thinking she could use the blood of the dying Centaur Nessus(半人马) as a love - charm, unwittingly poisoned Hercules. unwittingly done this, his wife hung herself. In great pain, Hercules built a funeral pile and ascended Mount Œta. He gave his bow and arrows to Philoctetes and laid himself on the pile. When the flames consumed his mortal part, his divine part ascended to the heavens. He was then married to Hebe, the daughter of Juno.
Ganymede: Ganymede, a Trojan boy, was so beautiful that Jupiter, in the guise of an eagle, seized him and carried him off to heaven. There, he became the cup - bearer to the gods, replacing Hebe in this role. Hercules：赫拉克勒斯（Hercules）完成十二项功绩，因误杀友人沦为奴隶，后因妻子得伊阿尼拉误信半人马血为爱情魔药，致其中毒，妻子内疚自杀，赫拉克勒斯自焚升天成神，娶青春女神赫柏（Hebe）。 Ganymede：特洛伊王子甘尼美狄（Ganymede）因俊美被宙斯化为鹰掳至天庭为侍酒童。
Theseus: Theseus, the son of Ægeus, king of Athens, set out on a dangerous journey by land to reach his father's city, defeating various villains along the way. In Athens, he discovered that the city was forced to send seven youths and seven maidens every year to be devoured by the Minotaur, a monster kept in a labyrinth(迷宫) built by Dædalus. Theseus volunteered to be one of the victims and, with the help of Ariadne, Minos' daughter, who gave him a sword and a clew of thread, he killed the Minotaur and escaped the labyrinth. However, on his return journey, he forgot to change the black sails of the ship to white as promised to his father, causing his father to believe he was dead and commit suicide. Theseus then became the king of Athens. He also had an adventure with his friend Pirithous when they went to the underworld to kidnap the wife of Pluto, but they were captured and only rescued by Hercules.
Dædalus: Dædalus, a skilled artificer, built the labyrinth for King Minos. But later, he was imprisoned. He made wings for himself and his son Icarus using feathers and wax. Before the flight, he warned Icarus not to fly too high or too low. During the flight, Icarus, excited by the experience, flew too close to the sun. The heat melted the wax, and he fell into the sea and drowned. Dædalus managed to reach Sicily safely and built a temple to Apollo there, hanging up his wings as an offering. Theseus：忒修斯（Theseus）杀米诺斯牛首人身怪弥诺陶洛斯，借阿里阿德涅的线团走出迷宫，后因忘换船帆致父亲自杀，成为雅典王，与好友皮里托俄斯下冥界欲抢冥后，被赫拉克勒斯救出。 Dædalus：代达罗斯（Dædalus）为米诺斯建迷宫，因助忒修斯出逃被囚禁，制蜡翼携子伊卡洛斯（Icarus）逃亡，伊卡洛斯因飞近太阳蜡融坠海，代达罗斯至西西里，将儿子葬于伊卡利亚岛。
Bacchus: Bacchus, the son of Jupiter and Semele, was persecuted(迫害) by Juno. After he discovered the culture of the vine（葡萄藤） and the art of making wine, he traveled to India. When he returned to Greece and tried to introduce his worship there, he was opposed by some princes. Pentheus, the king of Thebes, forbade the performance of Bacchus' religious rites. Bacchus showed his power by causing strange transformations to the crew of a ship that tried to kidnap him. When Pentheus persisted in his opposition, Bacchus led his followers to drive Pentheus mad. In the end, Pentheus was torn to pieces by his own mother and the other Bacchanals.
Ariadne: Ariadne, the daughter of King Minos, helped Theseus escape from the labyrinth. However, Theseus abandoned her on the island of Naxos. Bacchus found her there, consoled her, and made her his wife. As a marriage present, he gave her a golden crown. When she died, Bacchus threw the crown into the sky, and it became a constellation. Bacchus：酒神巴克斯（Bacchus）巡游印度归来，底比斯王彭透斯（Pentheus）禁止其崇拜，被酒神信徒（包括其母）撕成碎片。 Ariadne：阿里阿德涅（Ariadne）助忒修斯后被弃于纳克索斯岛，巴克斯娶其为妻，将她的冠冕化为北冕座。
Erisichthon was a profane man who dared to cut down an oak tree that was sacred to Ceres. When he ordered his servants to do it, the tree bled, and a nymph inside warned him of the coming punishment. Despite this, he continued his crime. Ceres, in anger, called on Famine to punish him. Famine entered Erisichthon's body and made him suffer from an insatiable hunger. He sold his daughter to get food, but his daughter was repeatedly saved by Neptune, who changed her form each time. Eventually, Erisichthon was so consumed by hunger that he ate his own limbs and died. 厄里西克同（Erisichthon）砍伐刻瑞斯的圣橡树，遭女神诅咒永受饥饿，他变卖女儿，女儿被海神化为鱼、鸟等多次逃脱，最终厄里西克同食己身而死。
Orpheus, the son of Apollo and Calliope, was a great musician whose music could charm even wild beasts and inanimate objects. His wife, Eurydice, was bitten by a snake and died shortly after their marriage. Grief - stricken, Orpheus decided to go to the underworld to retrieve her. He played his lyre so beautifully that he moved the hearts of Pluto and Proserpine. They agreed to let Eurydice return with him on the condition that he did not look back at her until they reached the upper world. However, as they were about to exit, Orpheus, overcome by doubt, looked back, and Eurydice was snatched back to the underworld. After this, Orpheus withdrew from the company of women. One day, he was attacked by the Thracian maidens, who were inspired by the rites of Bacchus. They killed him, and his head and lyre were thrown into the river Hebrus. His head continued to sing, and his lyre was placed among the stars by Jupiter. 俄耳甫斯（Orpheus）之妻欧律狄刻（Eurydice）被蛇咬死后，俄耳甫斯下冥界，以歌声打动冥王，获准带妻回阳间，却因回头望而永失爱人。他拒绝女色，被酒神信徒杀死，头颅与竖琴化为鸣唱的流水与天籁。 Arion: Arion, a famous musician, was on his way home from a musical contest in Sicily, where he had won the prize. On the ship, the sailors plotted to kill him and steal his wealth. Arion, aware of their plan, asked to sing a last song before his death. After singing, he jumped into the sea. Dolphins, attracted by his music, carried him safely to shore. When the ship arrived at Corinth, Arion revealed the sailors' crime to Periander, the king. The sailors were punished for their treachery. Ibycus: Ibycus, a pious poet, was on his way to the chariot races and musical competitions at the Isthmus of Corinth when he was attacked and killed by robbers. The murderers went uncaught until they attended a performance in a theater where the Furies were represented in a chorus. At that moment, a flock of cranes flew overhead, and one of the murderers exclaimed about them. His reaction made the others suspect him, and they were eventually caught and punished for their crime. Simonides: Simonides, a poet, was commissioned by Scopas, king of Thessaly, to write a poem celebrating his exploits. In the poem, Simonides included the deeds of Castor and Pollux. When it was time to be rewarded, Scopas gave him only half of the promised amount, saying that Castor and Pollux should pay for the part about them. Later, Simonides was called out of the banquet hall where Scopas and his guests were. Shortly after he left, the roof of the hall collapsed, killing everyone inside. It was believed that Castor and Pollux had saved Simonides. Sappho: Sappho was a famous poetess who was passionately in love with a youth named Phaon. When her love was not reciprocated, she threw herself from the promontory of Leucadia into the sea, hoping to be cured of her love. There is a legend that she was saved by a dolphin. Arion：诗人阿里翁（Arion）航海遇劫，唱罢跳海，海豚驮其上岸，劫船者被识破后遭惩罚。 Ibycus：诗人伊比库斯（Ibycus）被谋杀，凶手在观看戏剧时见鹤群惊呼，暴露身份伏法。 Simonides：诗人西摩尼得斯（Simonides）为斯寇帕斯王作诗，因含颂神内容获神助，躲过宫殿坍塌。 Sappho：女诗人萨福（Sappho）爱上法翁未果，从莱卡迪亚悬崖跃海，传说被海豚救起。
Endymion: Endymion was a beautiful youth who herded his flock on Mount Latmos. Diana, the moon goddess, saw him sleeping one night and was so taken by his beauty that she came down and kissed him. There is another story that Jupiter gave him the gift of eternal youth combined with eternal sleep. Diana took care of his flock, ensuring his sheep and lambs were safe from wild beasts.
Aurora and Tithonus: Aurora, the goddess of the dawn, fell in love with Tithonus, the son of Laomedon, king of Troy. She stole him away and asked Jupiter to grant him immortality. However, she forgot to ask for eternal youth. As time passed, Tithonus grew old. When his hair turned white, Aurora left him. Eventually, he became so weak that he could no longer move, and he was transformed into a grasshopper(蚱蜢). Endymion：恩底弥翁（Endymion）因俊美被月神阿尔忒弥斯爱上，宙斯赐其永恒睡眠与青春，长睡不醒。 Aurora and Tithonus：曙光女神奥罗拉（Aurora）爱上提托诺斯（Tithonus），求宙斯赐其永生，却忘求青春，提托诺斯衰老成蚱蜢。
The Trojan War erupted when Paris, a Trojan prince, was chosen to judge a beauty contest among Juno, Minerva, and Venus. He awarded the golden apple to Venus, who promised him Helen, the fairest woman, as a reward. Helen, wife of Menelaus (king of Sparta), eloped with Paris to Troy, prompting Menelaus and his brother Agamemnon (leader of the Greek alliance) to rally Greek forces against Troy.
The war dragged on for ten years, marked by heroic feats and tragedies. Key events included the death of Hector (Troy’s greatest warrior) at the hands of Achilles (Greece’s mightiest hero), and Achilles’ own death from a poisoned arrow to his heel—his only vulnerable spot.
The Greeks finally seized Troy using the "Wooden Horse (trojan horse)" stratagem: a giant wooden horse filled with Greek soldiers was left outside the city as a supposed offering to Minerva. The Trojans, believing the war was over, dragged the horse inside their walls. That night, the hidden soldiers emerged, opened the gates, and sacked the city.King Priam of Troy was slain, and his royal family scattered. Helen returned to Menelaus, her husband. After the war, Agamemnon, returning to his kingdom, was murdered by his wife Clytemnestra and her lover Ægisthus, who resented Agamemnon’s long absence and tyranny. Years later, Agamemnon’s son Orestes avenged his father by killing Clytemnestra and Ægisthus, though he was tormented by the Furies for matricide until acquitted by the Athenian court of Areopagus. 特洛伊王子帕里斯（Paris）因评判三女神美貌选维纳斯，获赠海伦，引发特洛伊战争。希腊联军用木马计破城，赫克托尔（Hector）战死，普里阿摩斯（Priam）被杀，海伦重回墨涅拉俄斯身边，阿伽门农归后被妻杀，其子俄瑞斯忒斯为父复仇。After the Trojan War, Ulysses (Odysseus) endures a decade of perilous wanderings to return home to Ithaca. His journey is marked by extraordinary trials: Lotus-Eaters: His men taste the lotus plant, losing all desire to return home, forcing Ulysses to drag them back to the ship. Cyclops Polyphemus: Ulysses and his crew are trapped in the cave of the one-eyed giant Polyphemus. To escape, Ulysses blinds the giant, telling him his name is “Noman” to avoid retribution. Polyphemus curses him, invoking Poseidon’s wrath, which prolongs Ulysses’ journey. Æolus: The wind-god Æolus gifts Ulysses a bag containing all storm winds, but his crew, mistaking it for treasure, opens it, unleashing storms that drive them off course. Læstrygonians: These giant cannibals destroy all but Ulysses’ ship, devouring his men. Circe: The sorceress turns Ulysses’ crew into swine. With help from Hermes, Ulysses resists her magic, and she restores his men, hosting them for a year before advising them on their next steps. Sirens, Scylla, and Charybdis: Ulysses plugs his crew’s ears with wax to resist the Sirens’ alluring songs, while he is tied to the mast to listen. They then navigate past Scylla (a six-headed monster that devours six men) and Charybdis (a deadly whirlpool). Helios’ Cattle: Ulysses’ men, ignoring warnings, slaughter the sacred cattle of the sun-god Helios. As punishment, Zeus destroys their ship with a thunderbolt, killing all but Ulysses, who clings to wreckage and drifts to Calypso’s island. Calypso, a nymph, detains Ulysses for seven years, offering him immortality, but he pines for home. Zeus finally orders her to release him, and Ulysses builds a raft to sail away.A storm washes Ulysses ashore in Scheria, home of the Phæacians—skilled seafarers favored by the gods. Nausicaa, the king’s daughter, finds him and directs him to the palace. King Alcinoüs and Queen Arete welcome him, and after he recounts his adventures, they provide a ship to carry him to Ithaca, loaded with gifts.Upon returning to Ithaca, Ulysses, disguised as a beggar by Athena, discovers his palace overrun by 108 suitors vying for his wife Penelope’s hand, squandering his wealth. Penelope, faithful but pressured, proposes a contest: whoever strings Ulysses’ bow and shoots an arrow through twelve axes will marry her.Ulysses, still in disguise, succeeds easily. With help from his son Telemachus, loyal swineherd Eumæus, and cowherd Philoetius, he reveals himself and slaughters all the suitors. He then reunites with Penelope, who tests his identity by mentioning their secret bed (built around an olive tree), confirming it is truly him. The household is restored, and Ulysses reclaims his throne. 奥德修斯（Ulysses）返乡途中遇独眼巨人、食莲者、女巫喀耳刻等，用计通过塞壬、斯库拉与卡律布狄斯，船只因冒犯太阳神被毁，独留一命，经腓埃基亚人助其返乡，扮乞丐射死全部求婚者，与妻珀涅罗珀团聚。After the fall of Troy, Æneas, a Trojan prince and son of Venus, escapes the burning city with his father Anchises, son Iulus, and a band of survivors. Guided by the gods to found a new homeland (later Rome), they endure a series of trials: Harpies: These bird-like monsters curse them to hunger so intense they will “eat their tables,” a prophecy fulfilled when they consume bread laid on leaves. Carthage: Æneas is shipwrecked in Carthage, where Queen Dido falls deeply in love with him. They live together as lovers, but Jupiter sends Mercury to remind Æneas of his destiny to found Rome. He departs, and Dido, heartbroken, builds a funeral pyre and kills herself, cursing eternal enmity between Carthage and Rome. Æneas consults the Sibyl of Cumæ, who guides him to the Underworld to seek wisdom from his father Anchises. There, he witnesses the realms of the dead: Tartarus: Where sinners (e.g., Tityus, Sisyphus) endure eternal punishment. Elysium: The abode of the blessed, where Anchises reveals the future of Rome—its great leaders (Romulus, Augustus) and its destiny to rule the world. He shows Æneas the souls of his unborn descendants, emphasizing their role in building an empire of law and virtue. Upon reaching Italy, Æneas is welcomed by King Latinus, who, following an oracle, offers his daughter Lavinia in marriage. This angers Turnus, king of the Rutulians, who is betrothed to Lavinia, sparking war. Juno, still hostile to Trojans, incites conflict by rousing Latinus’ queen and the Rutulians.Key allies join Turnus: the cruel Mezentius and his son Lausus, and Camilla, a warrior maiden devoted to Diana, renowned for her speed and skill with a bow. The Trojans gain support from Evander, king of the Arcadians, and his son Pallas.In the climactic battle, Æneas slays Lausus, then Mezentius, who fights to avenge his son. Camilla is killed by Arruns, a Trojan ally, and her death demoralizes the Rutulians. Finally, Æneas and Turnus duel. Turnus, wounded, begs for mercy, but Æneas, seeing Pallas’ belt around Turnus’ waist (a trophy), kills him.With Turnus dead, the war ends. Æneas marries Lavinia, founds the city of Lavinium, and lays the groundwork for Rome, fulfilling the gods’ prophecy. 埃涅阿斯（Æneas）逃离特洛伊后，经迦太基遇狄多（Dido），因神谕离去致狄多自焚。他下冥界见父亲，得知罗马未来，后在意大利与图尔努斯（Turnus）开战，杀图尔努斯建罗马城。
Deception of Jupiter by Juno: Juno, eager to aid the Greeks, used Venus’ magical girdle (Cestus) to inflame Jupiter’s love, tricking him into promising Greek victory. When Jupiter realized her ruse, he raged, but Juno defended herself by reminding him of her past support in his war against the Titans.
The Shield of Achilles: Vulcan forged a magnificent shield for Achilles, depicting intricate scenes of the heavens, cities at war and peace, farming, harvests, weddings, and festivals. The shield symbolized the fullness of human life and the fate of nations, reflecting Greek civilization’s values. Deception of Jupiter by Juno：赫拉为助希腊人，用美貌与维纳斯的腰带迷惑宙斯，使其应允希腊获胜，宙斯醒悟后震怒，赫拉以 “曾助宙斯抗父” 为由辩解。 The Shield of Achilles：赫菲斯托斯(Vulcan)为阿喀琉斯造盾，刻画天地、战争、农耕、婚礼等场景，象征希腊文明与命运。
Mars Ares and Venus Aphrodite had a secret affair. Venus’ husband Vulcan, a smith god, crafted an unbreakable net to trap them in the act. He invited the gods to witness their shame; all mocked the pair, and their scandal spread across Olympus, exposing the gods’ flawed passions. 战神玛尔斯（Mars）与爱神维纳斯（Venus）私通，被维纳斯丈夫赫菲斯托斯用网捕获，众神围观嘲笑，二人丑闻传遍天庭。This hymn praises Aphrodite (Venus) as the goddess of love and beauty, whose power enchants both gods and mortals. It celebrates her role in kindling desire, uniting lovers, and governing all romantic and marital bonds, emphasizing her ability to soften even the hardest hearts. 颂歌赞美阿佛洛狄忒的美丽与神力，她司掌爱情与美，能融化众神与凡人之心，促成婚姻与欲望。
Offspring of Earth and Heaven: Rhea Gaia (Earth) and Uranus (Heaven) bore the Titans and other giants. Uranus imprisoned their children, so Gaia urged Saturn Cronos (a Titan) to overthrow him, which he did. Offspring of Cronos and Rhea: Cronus, fearing a prophecy that his children would Offspring of Earth and Heaven：大地母神盖亚与天父乌拉诺斯生十二泰坦等，乌拉诺斯囚禁子女，盖亚助克洛诺斯（Cronos）推翻父亲。 Offspring of Cronos and Rhea：克洛诺斯与瑞亚生宙斯等，因怕被子推翻，吞噬子女，瑞亚藏宙斯，宙斯成年后迫其吐出兄妹。 The Defeat of Cronos by Zeus：宙斯联合泰坦战败克洛诺斯，成为众神之王，宇宙由三兄弟三分。 Medea, daughter of King Æetes of Colchis, instantly falls deeply in love with Jason when he arrives to retrieve the Golden Fleece. Tormented by her passion, she secretly meets Jason, where she confesses her love and pledges to help him overcome the deadly trials set by her father (yoking fire-breathing bulls, sowing dragon’s teeth, and defeating the armed warriors that spring forth). Despite knowing her aid means betraying her family and homeland, her 狂热 (feverish) love overrides all loyalty. She promises Jason magical charms to protect him, revealing the inner conflict between her devotion to him and the guilt of turning against her kin. 美狄亚对伊阿宋一见钟情，在与伊阿宋密会时，许诺助其取金羊毛，愿背叛父亲，展现狂热爱情与背叛的矛盾。
Camilla, a warrior maiden and devotee of Diana Artemis, fights alongside Turnus against Æneas and the Trojans in Italy. Renowned for her speed (said to run over water without sinking) and skill with a bow, she leads a band of warrior women. In battle, she proves fierce and agile, slaying many Trojans. However, she is ultimately killed by Arruns, who strikes her with a javelin while she is distracted. As she dies, her spirit is carried to Diana’s realm, reuniting with the goddess she served faithfully. 亚马逊女战士卡米拉（Camilla）助图尔努斯战埃涅阿斯，擅长箭术与奔跑，后被阿利亚斯用标枪射死，灵魂回归狄安娜身边。Tiresias: Tiresias, a Theban seer, is transformed into a woman for seven years after striking two mating snakes. When Jupiter and Juno argue over whether men or women feel more pleasure in love, Tiresias (having experienced both) sides with Jupiter, angering Juno, who blinds him. In compensation, Jupiter grants him the gift of prophecy. Echo and Narcissus: Echo, a nymph, is cursed by Juno to only repeat others’ words, robbing her of the ability to declare her love for Narcissus. The youth, vain and cold, rejects her, and Echo wastes away until only her voice remains. Narcissus, in turn, becomes obsessed with his own reflection in a pool, pining away until he is transformed into the narcissus flower. Hermaphroditus: The son of Hermes and Aphrodite, Hermaphroditus, while bathing, is embraced by the nymph Salmacis, who prays to be fused with him. The gods grant her wish, merging their bodies into one being with both male and female traits. This “hermaphrodite” becomes a symbol of unity between opposites. Tiresias：忒瑞西阿斯（Tiresias）因触怒朱诺，被变女身七年，后因评判男女情欲比例，被朱庇特赐预知能力。 Echo and Narcissus：回声女神厄科（Echo）因得罪朱诺，只能重复他人话语，爱上纳西索斯（Narcissus）被拒，憔悴成石，纳西索斯因迷恋自己倒影化为水仙花。 Hermaphroditus：赫马佛洛狄忒斯（Hermaphroditus）与水泽仙女结合，被神变为阴阳人，从此 “雌雄同体” 象征二者合一。 Tereus, Procne, and Philomela: Tereus, king of Thrace, marries Procne but lusts after her sister Philomela. He rapes Philomela, cuts out her tongue to silence her, and hides her away. Philomela weaves a tapestry revealing her ordeal, which Procne discovers. In revenge, Procne kills her and Tereus’ son, cooks him, and serves him to Tereus. When he learns the truth, he pursues the sisters, who are transformed into birds: Procne into a swallow, Philomela into a nightingale (her song echoing her silent suffering), and Tereus into a hoopoe. Tereus, Procne, and Philomela：特瑞俄斯（Tereus）娶普罗克涅（Procne），后奸污其妹菲罗墨拉（Philomela），割其舌，菲罗墨拉织锦帕诉冤，普罗克涅杀子烹食复仇，三人化为燕子、夜莺、戴胜鸟。 Daedalus and Icarus：代达罗斯与伊卡洛斯用蜡翼逃亡，伊卡洛斯因贪高蜡融坠海，代达罗斯至西西里，为纪念子，将岛命名 “伊卡利亚”。

item.title: Æthiopians(埃塞俄比亚人)
item.description: On the south side of the earth，close to the stream of Ocean(River Ocean)，dwelt a people happy and virtuous(善良的，道德高尚的) as the Hyperboreans ． They were named the Æthiopians．The gods favored them so highly that they were wont to leave at times their Olympian abodes and go to share their sacrifices and banquets.
item.guid: My notes/English/希腊罗马神话/subnotes/Æthiopians(埃塞俄比亚人).md

item.title: how the Sun Moon moves(日月星辰的运转)
item.description: The Dawn，the Sun，and the Moon were supposed to rise out of the Ocean，on the eastern side，and to drive through the air，giving light to gods and men．The stars，also，except those forming the Wain(北斗七星) or Bear（大熊座），and others near them，rose out of and sank into the stream of Ocean．There the sun⁃god embarked in a winged boat，which conveyed him round by the northern part of the earth，back to his place of rising in the east
item.guid: My notes/English/希腊罗马神话/subnotes/how the Sun Moon moves(日月星辰的运转).md

item.title: River Ocean
item.description: > the River Ocean，its course being from south to north on the western side of the earth，and in a contrary direction on the eastern side．It flowed in a steady，equable(温和的，稳定的) current，unvexed(不受干扰的) by storm or tempest．The sea，and all the rivers on earth，received their waters from it．
item.guid: My notes/English/希腊罗马神话/subnotes/River Ocean.md

item.title: Hyperboreans 北国人
item.description: The northern portion of the earth was supposed to be inhabited by a happy race named the Hyperboreans，dwelling in everlasting bliss and spring beyond the lofty mountains whose caverns were supposed to send forth the piercing(刺骨的) blasts of the north wind，which chilled the people of Hellas（希腊语Greece）．Their country was inaccessible by land or sea．They lived exempt from disease or old age，from toils and warfare．
item.guid: My notes/English/希腊罗马神话/subnotes/Hyperboreans 北国人.md
